notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 via read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes). Agent read SKILL.md and references/ then graded all 5 eval scenarios against documented behavior. Scores reflect SKILL.md coverage, not existing benchmark claims.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: searches for OAuth-related code patterns (refresh tokens, grants, state parameter)
assertions.note: SKILL.md Category 1 (Account Authentication Persistence) explicitly covers OAuth refresh tokens, grants, and state parameters.

assertions.id: checks token invalidation on account disable/password change
assertions.note: Category 1 vector includes token invalidation on account disable/password change as a required check.

assertions.id: reports token logging exposure risk
assertions.note: Category 2 (Credential Persistence) covers token logging exposure; skill requires classifying per vector.

assertions.id: classifies each item with evidence (file:line citations for COVERED)
assertions.note: SKILL.md output format requires COVERED/PARTIAL/MISSING with file:line citations for all COVERED items.

assertions.id: generates remediation list ordered by severity
assertions.note: SKILL.md requires remediation list but severity-ordering is implied by the CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM structure, not explicitly mandated for the ordering of the remediation items.

runs.notes: Strong coverage for generic OAuth repo audit. All 6 categories covered per SKILL.md. Minor gap: severity-ordered remediation is implied by the output format but not explicitly stated as a sort requirement.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: loads references/hydra-persistence-vectors.md
assertions.note: SKILL.md explicitly instructs loading references/hydra-persistence-vectors.md when 'hydra' argument is passed.

assertions.id: checks per-persona PAT scoping (builder vs reviewer vs security)
assertions.note: H1 vector requires checking per-persona PAT isolation and scope boundaries with file:line citations.

assertions.id: audits yolo auto-merge trust chain
assertions.note: H4 vector covers yolo auto-merge trust chain with full checklist: label authorization, merge race, signature verification.

assertions.id: checks label state machine integrity and reconciliation window
assertions.note: H5 vector covers label integrity including atomic swap verification and 10-min reconciler window.

assertions.id: checks log integrity and token masking in pipeline logs
assertions.note: H6 vector explicitly covers log integrity and token masking in pipeline logs.

assertions.id: reports on container isolation boundaries
assertions.note: H3 covers most container isolation items but USER directive enforcement in Dockerfiles requires explicit grep verification that may not always be done.

runs.notes: Hydra-specific audit is comprehensive via the references/hydra-persistence-vectors.md reference. Container isolation is mostly covered; USER directive verification is a known gap.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: uses heightened severity: PARTIAL items appear in critical gaps list
assertions.note: SKILL.md 'post-incident' argument triggers heightened mode where PARTIAL is treated as MISSING in severity scoring.

assertions.id: checks whether credential rotation actually revoked all trust paths
assertions.note: Post-incident checklist includes verifying rotation revoked all trust paths (OAuth tokens, deploy keys, machine identities).

assertions.id: verifies audit trail integrity (could evidence have been tampered with?)
assertions.note: Post-incident mode adds audit trail integrity check: log signing, append-only storage, evidence tampering.

assertions.id: recommends re-audit after remediation
assertions.note: Post-incident output format includes 're-audit after remediations' as a required follow-up action.

assertions.id: suggests verifying COVERED items are enforced, not just documented
assertions.note: Follow-up Actions include verification steps, but skill cannot perform these itself without external API calls — appropriately deferred to human with specific guidance.

runs.notes: Post-incident mode heightened sensitivity is well-defined. The COVERED-vs-enforced verification is correctly deferred to human follow-up with specific instructions.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: reads openspec/ specs and architecture documents
assertions.note: SKILL.md 'spec' argument mode directs the audit to openspec/ directory including architecture/ and specs/.

assertions.id: identifies which persistence vectors are addressed in the design
assertions.note: Spec audit classifies vectors as COVERED when design documents explicitly address them with spec file:section citations.

assertions.id: flags vectors not mentioned in specs as MISSING
assertions.note: Vectors not found in design documents are correctly classified as MISSING with justification.

assertions.id: recommends code-level follow-up audit
assertions.note: Follow-up Actions recommend code-level audit, but skill could be more explicit that spec-COVERED ≠ implementation-verified.

runs.notes: Spec-only audit handles the constraint correctly. Minor gap: skill could more clearly distinguish 'spec addresses it' from 'implementation verified' in COVERED classifications.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: does not fabricate findings for non-existent attack surfaces
assertions.note: SKILL.md output format requires N/A classification with clear reasoning when a category has no applicable attack surface.

assertions.id: marks OAuth, service accounts, IdP as N/A with clear reasoning
assertions.note: SKILL.md instructs N/A with explicit justification (e.g., 'No authentication implemented') rather than empty/skipped entries.

assertions.id: still checks CI/CD (GitHub Actions may have secrets) and endpoint persistence
assertions.note: SKILL.md requires all 6 categories to be checked. CI/CD (Category 5) and endpoint persistence (Category 6) are checked even when other categories are N/A.

assertions.id: completes all 6 categories — does not skip any
assertions.note: SKILL.md requires all 6 categories but the N/A classification itself depends on the skill recognizing which categories apply. Without explicit N/A guardrails, a minimal surface could tempt the skill to skip silent categories.

runs.notes: N/A handling is defined in SKILL.md output format. The completeness requirement (all 6 categories) is a guardrail. Minor gap: N/A guardrails could be more explicit for minimal-surface repos.
